problem.summary: Apply a single 2D five-point stencil pass to a row-major float grid. The baseline reads all neighbors from global memory; the optimized version stages a block tile plus halo in shared memory.
outputs: output contains one weighted five-point stencil pass with boundary cells copied from input
constraints: width and height may not be multiples of the tile dimensions
input and output do not alias
single pass only; no inter-iteration synchronization is required

verification.method: Harness compares device output against a CPU five-point stencil reference with exact coefficients.

curation.notes: No GPU timing has been collected. The shared-memory variant is a scaffold for measuring reuse and halo overhead, not a claimed win.
